title: Involution: (theta')' = theta in Dr(M), with correct tropical scaling normalization

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
Prove $\Phi^2=\mathrm{id}$, i.e. $(\theta')'=\theta$ as elements of $\Dr(M)$.
Pointwise $(\theta')'(F)=\theta'(F^\perp)=\theta((F^\perp)^\perp)=\theta(F)$ uses
$(F^\perp)^\perp=F$. However elements of $\Dr(M)$ are valuated matroids **up to tropical scaling**,
so one must verify the global additive constant is handled consistently and that equality holds
on the nose after fixing a normalization (e.g. pinning $\theta$ on a chosen flat). Confirm no
scaling ambiguity breaks involutivity.
